notes: Thirty-third validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its mission says the school creates a diverse educational community grounded in Montessori philosophy that fosters curiosity, creativity, and critical thinking.
Official materials say the school is governed by the Laura Holland Uzzell Foundation, a nonprofit established in 1993.
The school says students work in a prepared environment that promotes freedom within limits.
Official materials say Montessori classrooms use three-year multi-age groupings to build communities where older children share knowledge with younger ones.
Administration pages list Elizabeth Uzzell as Interim Executive Director.
Board leadership pages identify Rachel Richardson as president.
Official materials say the school creatively teaches Common Core curriculum, foundational business principles, and key elements of entrepreneurial success.
Its stated goal is to develop independent thinkers and positive, productive citizens through character development and self-enhancement opportunities.
The school highlights its MicroSociety Program as part of its whole-child approach.
Official history says the school was founded in August 2000.
Staff pages identify Shadrach Martin as principal.
Administration pages list Tenna Williams as principal while staff pages list her as executive director.
Its mission says the school provides a well-rounded education through rigorous, innovative, and inquiry-based instruction.
Official materials describe the school as a member of the Charter Schools USA family of schools.
The school's principal message says its 2025-2026 schoolwide initiative is RESET.
Official gifted-program materials say the school is one of only two charter schools in North Carolina recognized as a Promising District.
The school calls itself Home of the Rockets.
Principal's message pages identify Rebecca Feliciano as principal.
Its public-facing tagline is Better Education for a Better World.
Official materials say the school focuses on American Values alongside inquiry-based learning.
The school says its academic program uses the Core Knowledge Sequence and a values-based character education program built on Core Virtues.
Official messaging says the school aims to care for both the hearts and minds of students.
The school says its goal is to develop content knowledge, civic duty, and personal pride in future leaders.
Its official site prominently tells prospective families, "We saved a seat...for YOU!"
Its mission says the school develops productive citizens through a challenging classical academic program, meaningful parental partnerships, character education, and student responsibility.
Official materials say the school opened in August 2023.
Board materials say the governing board is ultimately responsible to the Office of Charter Schools and State Board of Education for sound operations.
Official pages say regular board meetings are usually held on the fourth Tuesday of the month and are open to the public.
The board page lists Bernie Parnell as chairman.
The school's mission uniquely combines a classical academic program with explicit emphasis on parental partnerships and character education.
